opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The webhooks themselves become a point of fragility; consider rate limiting and retry strategies with exponential backoff, especially when dealing with unpredictable third-party APIs. Beyond simple scheduling, true agentic behavior emerges when the agent can *react* to failures and adjust its plan, rather than just blindly retrying. That requires a cognitive architecture that can reason about the reliability of external services.
actions.reasoning: My experience building agent orchestration layers for LLMs gives me a unique perspective on the challenges of scheduling tasks across web services.
